title: <mark>Dendrite</mark> initiation and deflection in biaxially stressed solid electrolytes
abstract: Published online: 01 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10734-x By deflecting <mark>dendrite</mark> propagation, in-plane biaxial compression provided direct evidence that <mark>dendrite</mark> initiation occurs within the interior of <mark>garnet</mark> solid electrolytes during long-term cycling.

title: Electrochemical Sensors for Heavy Metals: Progress, Hurdles, and Future Directions
authors: Babankumar S. Bansod and Monika Antil
abstract: Heavy metal contamination remains a critical global challenge with serious implications for environmental <mark>safety</mark> and public health. Electrochemical sensing techniques have emerged as powerful tools for heavy metal detection owing to their high sensitivity, portability, rapid response, and cost-effectiveness. Despite significant progress, translating laboratory-scale electrochemical sensors into reliable, field-deployable systems remains constrained by challenges such as poor selectivity in complex matrices, electrode fouling, reproducibility issues, and the need for extensive sample pretreatme…
